name: Nat Pierce
abstract: Nathaniel Pierce Blish Jr., known professionally as Nat Pierce (July 16, 1925 – June 10, 1992) was an American jazz pianist and prolific composer and arranger, perhaps best known for being pianist and arranger for the Woody Herman band from 1951 to 1955. Pieces by Pierce were predominantly created for use in big bands.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Chicago Jazz Sextet

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: Nat Pierce, Chuck Anderson and me. They’re both dead now... But that’s all there was, three guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: Nat Pierce and Chase was there every night, through the whole -- and if we got different trumpet players at different times, they were usually guys from Boston or New York area, who'd been in the band

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: He had a band... Nat Pierce was the pianist... Frankie Capp was drummer... I did work with Neal Hefti... We worked Disney band

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Nat Pierce was playing piano... I joined the band... I ended up staying... it was just a year I was there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: Woody Herman was cool except that Woody could be really icy cold at times you know, and you know I’ve seen him downgrade Sal Nestico when we were on the band together. And Nat Pierce wrote a ballad for Sal... and Woody wouldn’t let him play it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Buckingham
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions 'Frankie Capp and Nat Pierce' in the context of hearing the L.A. Juggernaut Big Band; the interviewee does not mention Nat Pierce or indicate any personal interaction.
